doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve been having this really severe headache for the past two days. It feels different from my usual migraines, like a band-like pressure in my forehead, and it’s just… it’s not going away. I’m kinda worried, to be honest.

doctor_turn: When did this headache first start?
patient_turn: It started about two days ago, um, and it’s just been getting worse since then. I’ve tried taking Tylenol and Advil, which helped a little bit at first, but then the pain just came back.

doctor_turn: Where exactly do you feel the pain, and does it spread to any other areas?
patient_turn: It’s mostly in my forehead, like a band around my head, you know? It doesn’t really spread anywhere else, just that pressure in the front. It’s, um, definitely different from my usual migraines.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the pain? Is it sharp, dull, or more like pressure?
patient_turn: Well, it’s kind of like this pressure, I guess, but not the same as my usual migraines. You know, I had a headache like this once before, but it was different. And, oh, by the way, I’ve been drinking a lot of water lately, trying to stay hydrated, which is important, right? And I also had pizza last week, which I think really hit the spot.

doctor_turn: What have you noticed that makes the pain better or worse?
patient_turn: Um, like I said, Tylenol and Advil helped a little bit at first, but it didn’t last long. I haven’t really noticed anything else that makes it better or worse, honestly. It just seems to be there all the time, which is really frustrating.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the pain? Is it sharp, dull, or more like pressure?
patient_turn: Oh, it’s definitely sharp, I think, but I don’t usually get sharp pains, so maybe I’m wrong about that. I mean, last time it felt more like pressure, but I’m not really sure what it is this time.

doctor_turn: What does the headache feel like? Is it sharp, dull, or more like pressure?
patient_turn: It feels more like pressure, I guess, but it’s also kind of sharp at times, which is confusing. It’s definitely not like my usual migraines, which are different, you know? This one just feels… heavier, I think.

doctor_turn: Has the headache changed in intensity or character since it first started?
patient_turn: I’ve been meaning to ask, do you think they’ll have cookies in the waiting room later? I really hope so!

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any nausea, vomiting, or changes in your vision?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any nausea or vomiting, and my vision seems fine, I think. It’s just this headache and, um, I’ve been feeling really tired and a bit short of breath, but no other symptoms like that.

doctor_turn: Can you describe your shortness of breath? Does it happen all the time or only with activity?
patient_turn: Um, it’s not all the time, but I’ve noticed it a bit, especially when I’m moving around or, like, doing anything active. It’s kind of mild, I guess, but it’s just… there, you know? It’s making me a little anxious, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any muscle aches along with your headache?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’ve had some mild muscle aches too, which started around the same time as the headache. It’s just, um, everything feels a bit off, you know? I’m not used to feeling like this, and it’s kind of worrying me.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any fever, chills, or chest pain recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any fever or chills, and, um, no chest pain either. It’s just the headache, the muscle aches, and feeling tired, you know? I’m really hoping it’s nothing serious.

doctor_turn: Are you currently taking any medications for your headaches or other health issues?
patient_turn: Um, no, I’m not taking any medications right now. I’ve had migraines in the past, but I haven’t been on anything for them lately. I just, you know, try to manage it with over-the-counter stuff when it happens.

doctor_turn: Have you been hospitalized or had any surgeries in the past?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t been hospitalized or had any surgeries before. I mean, I’ve had migraines and, um, I have polymyalgia rheumatica, but nothing major like that. So, I guess I’m pretty lucky in that regard.

doctor_turn: Do you smoke, drink alcohol, or use any recreational drugs?
patient_turn: No, I don’t smoke or drink alcohol, and I don’t use any recreational drugs either. I’ve just been trying to stay healthy, you know? It’s, um, important to me, especially with how I’ve been feeling lately.

doctor_turn: Is there any family history of heart disease or cancer in your relatives?
patient_turn: Um, no, there’s not really a significant family history of heart disease or cancer that I know of. My family has some migraines, but, you know, nothing major like that. I’m just hoping it’s not anything serious.

doctor_turn: Have you dined out with anyone who was feeling unwell recently?
patient_turn: I recently dined out with friends, but, um, they weren’t known to be ill. I mean, I didn’t notice anyone looking sick or anything like that. It’s just been a bit strange how I started feeling unwell after that.

doctor_turn: Have you traveled anywhere recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t traveled anywhere recently. I’ve just been, um, working from home because of how I’ve been feeling. It’s kind of frustrating, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Are you currently working from home because of your symptoms?
patient_turn: Yeah, I am. I’ve been working from home because of my symptoms, and it’s, um, really hard to focus with this headache and everything else going on. I just wish I could feel normal again.

doctor_turn: Do you have any known allergies to medications?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any known drug allergies. I mean, I haven’t had any issues with medications before, so that’s good, I guess. It’s just, um, I’m really hoping to figure out what’s going on with me.
